title: wolfhsm: key operation methods take &mut Client ergonomics
description: All methods on key types (EccP256Key, Ed25519Key, etc.) take client: &mut Client as a parameter. A RustCrypto user expects either the key type to carry a borrow of the client (struct EccP256Key<'a> { client: &'a mut Client, id: KeyId }) so operations are self-contained, or for operations to be methods on Client. The current shape forces callers to juggle mutable borrows at every call site and reads backwards (key.sign(&mut client, msg) rather than client.sign(key, msg) or key.sign(msg)). Evaluate and document the chosen convention, or change it.
